div1.entryFree: Prodigy, portent, ominous apparition: the people are amazed at apparitions, signs and --es, Ven. 926. as if they saw some wondrous monument, some comet or unusual p. Shr. III, 2, 98. call them meteors, --es and signs, John III, 4, 157. now hath my soul brought forth her p. R2 II, 2, 64 (her monstrous birth). a p. of fear and a portent of mischief, H4A V, 1, 20. where's that valiant crook-back p.? H6C I, 4, 75. disturbed with --es, Tit. I, 101. when these --es do so conjointly meet, Caes. I, 3, 28. these apparent --es, II, 1, 198.
